scenario.title: The Deployment Planner
scenario.context: You're ready to deploy your AI-assisted project. You need to create a deployment checklist and environment configuration that AI can help verify and troubleshoot.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that guides AI to create a comprehensive deployment checklist and environment setup for a specific project type.
constraints: Cover all deployment phases (build, test, deploy, verify)
scenario.exampleInput: Deploy a Next.js app with Supabase backend to Vercel. Uses environment variables for API keys, has database migrations, and needs preview deployments for PRs.
hints: Specify your tech stack and hosting platform
List what needs to happen before, during, and after deploy
Think about what could go wrong at each step

rubric.name: Stack Context
rubric.description: Is the tech stack clearly specified?

scoringGuide.excellent: Complete stack with versions and hosting details
Build, test, deploy, verify, rollback all covered

rubric.name: Environment Configuration
rubric.description: Are environment variables and configs addressed?

scoringGuide.excellent: Env vars, secrets management, per-environment config

rubric.name: Failure Handling
rubric.description: Are failure scenarios and rollback addressed?

scoringGuide.excellent: Common failures listed with rollback procedures

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Create a deployment checklist for this project:

DEPLOYMENT PLAN: Next.js + Supabase → Vercel

---

PROJECT STACK:

```yaml
framework: Next.js 14 (App Router)
runtime: Node.js 20.x
database: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
auth: Supabase Auth
hosting: Vercel
ci_cd: GitHub Actions + Vercel Git Integration

environment_variables:
  - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (public)
  - NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (public)
  - SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (secret)
  - DATABASE_URL (secret, for migrations)
  - NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL (per-environment)
```

---

PRE-DEPLOYMENT CHECKLIST:

```yaml
code_quality:
  - [ ] All tests passing locally
  - [ ] No TypeScript errors (npm run build)
  - [ ] No lint errors (npm run lint)
  - [ ] No console.logs in production code
  - [ ] Error boundaries in place

database:
  - [ ] Migrations tested on staging
  - [ ] RLS policies reviewed
  - [ ] Indexes for slow queries
  - [ ] Backup taken before destructive migrations

environment:
  - [ ] All env vars set in Vercel dashboard
  - [ ] Preview env vars configured
  - [ ] Production secrets rotated recently
  - [ ] NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL correct per environment

dependencies:
  - [ ] No security vulnerabilities (npm audit)
  - [ ] Lock file committed (package-lock.json)
  - [ ] No unused dependencies
```

---

DEPLOYMENT PHASES:

```yaml
phase_1_build:
  trigger: Push to main or PR
  steps:
    - Vercel clones repo
    - Installs dependencies (npm ci)
    - Runs build (next build)
    - Outputs static + serverless functions
  verification:
    - Build logs show no errors
    - Bundle size within limits
    - All routes generated

phase_2_database:
  when: If migrations pending
  steps:
    - Connect to Supabase via DATABASE_URL
    - Run: npx supabase db push (or migration script)
    - Verify schema matches expected
  verification:
    - No migration errors
    - Tables created/modified correctly
    - RLS policies applied

phase_3_deploy:
  steps:
    - Vercel deploys to edge network
    - Serverless functions deployed
    - Static assets cached
  verification:
    - Deployment URL accessible
    - No 500 errors on key routes

phase_4_verify:
  steps:
    - Hit health check endpoint
    - Test auth flow
    - Test database connection
    - Test key user journeys
  verification:
    - /api/health returns 200
    - Can login/logout
    - Can read/write to database
```

---

ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION:

```typescript
// Vercel Environment Setup

Production (main branch):
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: https://xxx.supabase.co
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: eyJ...
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: eyJ... (encrypted)
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: https://myapp.com
  NODE_ENV: production

Preview (PR branches):
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: https://xxx.supabase.co (staging project)
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: eyJ... (staging key)
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: eyJ... (staging, encrypted)
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: $VERCEL_URL (automatic)
  NODE_ENV: production

Local (.env.local):
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: http://localhost:54321
  NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: local-anon-key
  SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: local-service-key
  NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: http://localhost:3000
```

---

COMMON FAILURES & FIXES:

```yaml
build_failures:
  - error: "Module not found"
    cause: Missing dependency or wrong import path
    fix: Check package.json, verify import paths
  
  - error: "Type error"
    cause: TypeScript strict mode catching issues
    fix: Run `npm run build` locally first
  
  - error: "Out of memory"
    cause: Large bundle or too many pages
    fix: Increase Node memory or optimize imports

runtime_failures:
  - error: "Environment variable undefined"
    cause: Missing env var in Vercel dashboard
    fix: Add to Vercel project settings
  
  - error: "Database connection failed"
    cause: Wrong DATABASE_URL or Supabase down
    fix: Verify URL, check Supabase status
  
  - error: "Auth redirect loop"
    cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL mismatch
    fix: Set correct URL for environment
```

---

ROLLBACK PROCEDURE:

```yaml
if_deployment_fails:
  vercel:
    - Go to Vercel dashboard → Deployments
    - Find last working deployment
    - Click "..." → "Promote to Production"
    - Instant rollback (no rebuild)

  database:
    - If migration caused issue:
      - Create rollback migration (reverse changes)
      - Push rollback migration
      - Or restore from backup if data loss

  full_rollback:
    1. Rollback Vercel deployment
    2. Rollback database migration
    3. Verify app works on previous version
    4. Investigate root cause before retry
```

---

POST-DEPLOYMENT:

```yaml
monitoring:
  - Check Vercel analytics for errors
  - Monitor Supabase dashboard for query issues
  - Set up alerts for 5xx errors

documentation:
  - Update changelog
  - Note any manual steps required
  - Update runbook if new failure modes found
```
